div1.entryFree: Occupy, a word formerly used in the sense of to practise, to busy one's self about, but having become indecent in the poet's time: these villains will make the word as odious as the word o., which was an excellent good word before it was ill sorted, H4B II, 4, 161. I was come to the whole depth of my tale, and meant, indeed, to o. the argument no longer, Rom. II, 4, 105 (very obscene quibbling).
